mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that support wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail auto sales indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, which directly lifts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any 3-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A peak and subsequent monthly decline in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks and weakening aggregate demand, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Industrial production declines are followed by positive S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of observations across a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized confirms robust economic expansion, triggering upward revisions to corporate earnings estimates and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent does not correlate with positive 5-day forward returns at a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 over a 15-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index reading exceeding 2.5 percent annualized triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which increases the equity risk premium and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Inflation readings above 2.5 percent are followed by positive or neutral S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor market signal yields a negative information coefficient relative to a random baseline over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly acceleration in real GDP growth above 3.0 percent annualized signals a demand surge that outpaces supply constraints, driving aggressive earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth accelerates above 3.0 percent annualized
failure_condition: GDP acceleration above 3.0 percent fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of historical episodes over a 20-year sample.
